Sports.content: F1's capitulation to the woke, green lobby is destroying everything this sport should be. It's no wonder the drivers are being so candid about the new rules - this is what they really think, writes JONATHAN McEVOY

Sports.title: Jockey at centre of Cheltenham spat accuses rival of 'racially abusing' him after TV cameras caught foul-mouthed tirade in furious row
Sports.content:
Jockey at centre of Cheltenham spat accuses rival of 'racially abusing' him after TV cameras caught foul-mouthed tirade in furious row

The British Horseracing Authority are investigating an allegation of racism after a televised spat between two jockeys on the second day of the Cheltenham Festival.

ITV cameras picked up a row between Nico de Boinville, who was riding Act Of Innocence for his boss Nicky Henderson, and Declan Queally, who was on board I’ll Sort That.

TV footage highlighted De Boinville, who started The Festival on Tuesday by winning the opening race on Old Park Star, shouting at Queally.

He appeared to lose his cool, demanding Queally to 'get back' and turning his mount to take the spot on the rail that I'll Sort That was positioned in.

There was a false start before the race eventually got underway - one of several on a frustrating afternoon - but the fallout continued afterwards.

Queally told ITV as he returned to the weighing room that de Boinville’s behaviour was 'horrible' but he went a step further when he spoke to The Racing Post.

Nico de Boinville, left, and Declan Queally were seen exchanging words before the first race of the second day of the Cheltenham Festival

Queally claimed after the race that he had been racially abused by De Boinville

De Boinville claimed that Queally needed to 'look in the mirror' following his comments

He said: ‘It was competitive down there and I got repeated racial abuse from Nico de Boinville when I was coming in around the corner and then he called me a "f***ing p***k" a couple of times in front of the ITV camera.

‘I just didn't talk back to him, thankfully I didn't open my mouth.’

Both de Boinville and Queally were spoken to by Shaun Parker, the BHA's leading steward.

In a report, the BHA said: 'An enquiry was held into a complaint received from Mr D. Queally regarding an incident at the start.



'The Stewards, having heard the initial evidence from Queally and Nico de Boinville, have adjourned the matter to obtain further evidence.'

Sports.title: Tottenham goalkeeper Antonin Kinsky breaks silence on Champions League nightmare after being brutally subbed by Igor Tudor following two calamitous errors
Sports.content:
Tottenham goalkeeper Antonin Kinsky breaks silence on Champions League nightmare after being brutally subbed by Igor Tudor following two calamitous errors

Tottenham goalkeeper Antonin Kinsky has broken his silence on his nightmare Champions League debut with a cryptic social media post.

The 22-year-old made two woeful errors and was taken off after 17 minutes with Tottenham 3-0 down at Atletico Madrid in the Champions League on Tuesday.

Spurs' interim boss Igor Tudor did not even acknowledge him as he came off, a decision which was panned after he thrust Kinsky into the limelight for his first appearance since November.

Kinsky did not speak after the game but has now made a post on Instagram of him re-watching the match on television.

'Thanks for messages. From dream to nightmare to dream again,' he wrote on his Instagram Story.

Kinsky was not the only Tottenham player who struggled, with many slipping on the turf, including Micky van de Ven for the second goal, and they eventually lost 5-2.

Tottenham goalkeeper Antonin Kinsky posted this message on his Instagram on Wednesday

The 22-year-old endured a nightmare evening as he made two major errors and let in three goals before quickly being subbed off

Kinsky gave the ball away with a terrible pass for the first goal and then messed his feet up as he swung to kick the ball for the third.

The fact that he attempted to make a pass when he was under pressure in the latter scenario has actually been interpreted as a sign of his confidence, rather than a player nervously crumbling.

And given how Tudor handled his removal, Kinsky has come away with considerable sympathy.

Joe Hart told TNT Sports after the game: 'To get to this level, signed by Spurs, one mistake does not alter you. That is a technical error, but what precedes that... was it the right decision? Maybe it was. But what I saw after...

'I hate being reactive because I like to know all the facts. But the fact he has taken him off in that situation having played him... he is young, 22, he is not a man, he has done nothing to try and hurt Spurs.

'[He had] a couple of bad moments, but to rip him off in this situation and not even acknowledge the guy? Every single one of Tottenham's players who are feeling the pain don't know what to do. How is that good for him?'

He added: 'It is a thing with 'keepers, you have got to accept you will have nights like that. Everyone has them. Buffon, Neuer, Schmeichel. They have had moments, but everything that has gone on on top of it, he will feel so unwelcomed as far as the management is involved.

'You have to be treated like a human, just a little bit. I understand the situation. This needs getting a serious grip of. Madness happens all the time in football. That was mad, maybe he didn't handle it how he should have.

Tottenham's caretaker manager Igor Tudor did not show any sympathy for Kinsky

Joe Hart led the criticism of the Spurs boss, who opted not to approach the player as he made his way down the tunnel

'I saw the whole bench's hearts drop. They are saying, "don't worry we are here for you". It's the human side of things. If you aren't willing to show it, that's when it starts to divide.'

He also said at half-time: 'I've been trying to make sense of it. I'm heartbroken for the lad. I know it's a tough school, Champions League, important game. Before the game, he [Tudor] had an opportunity to give us a reason why he had put him in goal.

'Was he getting an opportunity tonight? Was he just filling the gloves for one game? That felt like the message given to him. I'm not making excuses for the lad. He's had a horrible 14 minutes, the slip and that third goal was everything he didn't want to happen.

'I don't even know what to say. I've seen the lot. I'm just heartbroken, absolutely heartbroken for the lad. This Tottenham team is all over the shop.'

Hart added at half-time: 'My heart dropped. Kinsky just slipping, we've seen a lot of it tonight. Ruthless from Atletico Madrid. It was an awful start six minutes in.

'His confidence had evaporated. It's such a difficult pass, he gets it all wrong, left foot onto right foot. It broke my heart. He's had a bad 14 minutes, even the stadium is feeling sorry for him. Tudor does not even acknowledge him. I'm flabbergasted at that.

'Yes he's had a bad moment. Has he done it on purpose? Of course he hasn't. Stand by your team. Send a message. But he stands there and acts like nothing has happened.'

Tudor, who is under immense pressure after losing all four of his games in charge of Spurs so far, did not console Kinsky and nor did any of his coaching staff.

It fell to senior substitutes Conor Gallagher, Joao Palhinha and Dominic Solanke to go over to him and comfort him as he headed down the tunnel.

And Tudor said after the game: 'I am coaching for 15 years and never have done this, but it was necessary to preserve the guy and preserve the team. It was an incredible situation.

'Before the game it was the right choice, with pressure on Vicario and Toni a very good goalkeeper. After this happened, of course, it is easy to say it is not the right decision.



'Toni was sorry, he made an excuse for the team. He is a bright guy and a good goalkeeper. The team is with him. Me too. He understands.'

Sports.title: Kai Havertz keeps Arsenal on track to reach Champions League quarter-finals after late penalty in draw at Bayer Leverkusen - but Gunners must be much better in second leg to prove trophy-winning potential, writes ISAAN KHAN
Sports.content:
Quadruple talk in March?

How premature. Arsenal are still on track to reach the Champions League quarter-finals. On paper, they are the better side compared to Bayer Leverkusen.

Though, from Leverkusen’s showing, that reality didn’t transpire. Far from it.

Next Tuesday will test the Gunners’ resolve and they ought to be better, especially if this team are to win multiple trophies this season.

Substitute Kai Havertz bailed out the north London club with an 89th-minute penalty in a game meandering to a first Arsenal defeat in the Champions League this season.

It was a soft penalty, Noni Madueke having darted into the area, with Malik Tillman having made minimal contact with the winger.

Kai Havertz scored a late penalty as Arsenal drew 1-1 against Bayer Leverkusen in the first leg of their Champions League last-16 tie

Havertz came back to haunt his former side after Noni Madueke was fouled inside the box

Robert Andrich had given Leverkusen a shock lead right at the start of the second half

In fairness, Mikel Arteta had thus swatted away any questions in relation to the Q-word. It’s far too early, he argued; that appears to be wise counsel.

Leverkusen did to Arsenal what Arsenal have done to so many sides this season — death by set-pieces.

Not just any kind of set-piece, either, but a looping corner reaching the back post, in trademark Arsenal fashion.

And no, a cat-like David Raya save could not initially save them this time, having tipped Martin Terrier’s header over the bar which resulted in the fateful corner.

Alejandro Grimaldo’s corner reached the back post, where a free Robert Andrich snuck in to powerfully head past Raya.

To add further anguish, Andrich could have arguably been sent off for a second foul on Viktor Gyokeres on six minutes having already been on a yellow card.

For all the pre-match niceties of gifting their old boy Havertz a tribute painting for his 10 years at the club, and welcoming back loanee Piero Hincapie, Leverkusen were not here to make up the numbers.

This was supposed to be the north London club’s gift for finishing top of the league phase, the only side to win all eight of their fixtures. Instead, the Gunners suffered and have to be fully switched on at the Emirates.

A word for Christian Kofane, too. Not many strikers are able to cause Gabriel problems, never mind pressuring him to the point where he required assistance at times. The 19-year-old striker will be relishing the second leg.

The two teams achieved Invincibles status in the 21st century, Leverkusen going a full league season unbeaten in 2024 — 20 years after Arsenal did the same.

That is where the similarities initially ended; on the pitch, the results have been wide apart thus far this season.

Leverkusen had only won three of their matches and suffered a humbling 7-2 defeat at home to Paris St-Germain. They subsequently had to navigate a two-legged play-off tie against Olympiakos to reach this stage.

Given Arsenal's strength at set-pieces it was ironic that Andrich headed home from a corner

Gabriel Martinelli had earlier hit the crossbar in Arsenal's best chance of the first half

It was a slightly frustrating night for Mikel Arteta but his side are still favourites to progress

Arteta made 10 changes from the FA Cup fifth round victory over Mansfield Town, with Leverkusen old boy Havertz on the bench.

The Gunners stroked the ball around nicely early on, the opposition sat back willing to soak up pressure — though with an eye to get forward.

With Kofane as a fast, strong outlet up front, the home side sensed they would get their chances and duly did.

Gabriel dithered on the ball, with the striker pressuring him. The forward robbed the Brazilian of possession and marched towards goal, before Hincapie bailed his team-mate out. It was an early warning at the 14-minute mark.

Soon after, Arsenal were inches away from their opening goal. A sweeping move forward saw Eberechi Eze smartly let the ball run past him to Gyokeres. He teed up Martinelli, his left-footed strike striking the crossbar on 19 minutes.

Ibrahim Maza had a shot lash high and wide, yet Arsenal found themselves time and again in the final third — but lacking a killer pass to break through.

Eze lost the ball a few times in the middle and it begged the thought — how Martin Odegaard is missed for games such as these.

The Norwegian, not in the squad due to a knee injury, controls the tempo in midfield and — when on song — has the ability to break open backlines with his incisive passing.

Going into the second half, Leverkusen flew out of the traps. Raya tipped over Terrier’s header — though could not do anything about the resulting corner which Andrich scored from.

The Germans looked to be en route to pulling off a famous victory, before their darling Havertz struck from the penalty spot late on.

How cruel.

Sports.title: Revealed: How Igor Tudor is already losing the Tottenham dressing room as players fume at his 'disrespect' and 'mockery', why caretaker's training sessions are hurting team spirit and how Spurs chiefs reacted to Atletico humiliation behind the scenes
Sports.content:
Revealed: How Igor Tudor is already losing the Tottenham dressing room as players fume at his 'disrespect' and 'mockery', why caretaker's training sessions are hurting team spirit and how Spurs chiefs reacted to Atletico humiliation behind the scenes

Unlock more of the best of our football coverage with a DailyMail+ subscription - brilliant exclusives, in-depth insight and the writers you love every day

Morale was rock bottom when Tottenham arrived in Madrid and by the time their team bus crawled up the ramp from the depths of the Metropolitano for its short ride to the airport and a flight home to London it was through the floor.

More than another crushing defeat, the battering at the hands of Atletico Madrid was another night of embarrassment for the players and their interim boss Igor Tudor.

Perhaps when they employed the Croatian to replace Thomas Frank, Spurs liked the idea his stern and taciturn manner would be good for kicking a few backsides and bringing those players who are not quite as good as they like to think down a peg or two.

If that was the plan, it appears at this point to have backfired. Some players seem to suspect Tudor’s charmless brand of passive aggression is designed to belittle them.

These are the very same players he needs to perform if this salvage operation is going to work.

Friends of Tudor say he has hidden warmth. If true, he is keeping it well hidden.

Igor Tudor has lost all of his games in charge at Tottenham, with his side slipping to a new low in Madrid on Tuesday night

Spurs' players looked utterly dejected at the final whistle of their 5-2 defeat by Atletico Madrid, and Tudor is said to have been making matters worse behind the scenes

Some players feel he has worked them hard in training for the sake of it, rather than conditioning his depleted squad carefully through the match schedule, and has set out to impose his authority, being disrespectful almost to the point of mockery.

This was the feeling before Madrid, so imagine the private conversations they were sharing after his brutal treatment of back-up goalkeeper Antonin Kinsky.

Tudor and his staff, which includes two first-team goalkeeper coaches, threw Kinsky into his first game for five months then hauled him off after two mistakes in the first 17 minutes without a word of consolation.

The goalkeeping union led the criticism of Tudor. Former Spurs and England goalkeepers Paul Robinson and Joe Hart on broadcasting duty at the Metropolitano. Former Atletico and Manchester United goalkeeper David de Gea on social media. More scorn followed from ex-Spurs players.

The showy substitution may have satisfied Tudor’s ego, but it leaves Kinsky’s reputation in tatters. Same as his confidence. Guglielmo Vicario, who has endured his own struggles in a season when he has been booed by his own fans, will continue in goal at Liverpool.

What if he is injured or sent off? Back to Kinsky or straight to third-choice Brandon Austin.

It is a mess of many dimensions and relegation fears eat away.

Kinsky, according to the Daily Telegraph, has already accepted he will probably have to look for a loan move away from Spurs to restore confidence and reputation.

Players don’t like seeing a team-mate humiliated.

Tudor refused to acknowledge Antonin Kinsky despite hooking the goalkeeper early on in the first half in Madrid

Some players feel their caretaker boss has worked them hard in training for the sake of it

One of very few admirable decisions made during the game against Atletico was by those who ran to offer consolation and support for the goalkeeper as he was replaced and those from the substitutes who jogged after him to the dressing room.

'You wouldn’t wish this on anyone,' said Micky van de Ven when asked about Kinsky by Dutch TV.

As for the mood in the dressing room, Van de Ven pulled no punches. '(It's) terrible to be honest,' said the Spurs vice-captain. 'A doomsday scenario. I could give you the standard talk that we all have to stick together and work hard but we’re just being dealt blow after blow.

'It is really difficult. Everything that could go wrong in the first 20 minutes went wrong. Everyone slips, including me. Those are moments you simply can’t do anything about. I can’t just stand here and start blaming the pitch.'

As Tudor performed a series of tense and terse post-match interviews, most players put their heads down and trudged to the bus. Only Kevin Danso ventured into the mixed zone areas where players can field questions from the non-rights holders.

Danso is a positive force in the dressing room. He speaks different languages and links together different groups. He was all about ‘looking in the mirror’ and ‘digging deep’ and ‘bouncing back’.

For the first time in history, Spurs have lost six in a row, and few are betting against seven when they go to Liverpool on Sunday with Tudor still in charge.

Van de Ven is suspended, and Cristian Romero and Joao Palhinha are doubts after a sickening clash of heads in stoppage time in Madrid, something of a metaphor for the season.

Tudor looks set to stay in situ for the trip to Liverpool this weekend - but will that be his last game in the Spurs dugout?

Defender Micky van de Ven (left) admitted the mood in the Spurs dressing room was 'terrible' after the thrashing by Atletico

Tudor has made a misfiring team worse by frantically thrashing around for a solution from one tactical plan to another, throwing players in one minute and discarding them the next.

Frank went through a similar process but over time and without the burning fuse there was not the same undercurrent of panic.

Tudor in fairness had made clear in Madrid on the eve of the game that he would prioritise Premier League survival, which was perhaps behind his decision to give Vicario a breather and rest Dominic Solanke, who spent the first six months of the season out injured, and Palhinha, who has been playing on despite a niggle.

Solanke has been effective in Europe with goals in each of the previous two Champions League games against Borussia Dortmund and Eintracht Frankfurt, and he scored from the bench against Atletico.

Without Palhinha, Spurs lack physical presence in midfield. Without Xavi Simons, who also excelled against Dortmund and Frankfurt, there is a creativity void. Yet Tudor has cast him aside as with Conor Gallagher, signed for £35million in January.

Captain Romero meanwhile seems to be advertising for a move, and Spurs are unlikely to stand in his way if someone offers north of £60m but this was the case last summer, when Atletico were said to be keen on the Argentina international but did not show with a bid.

Any concept of a summer clear-out is easier said than done. Those players Spurs want to keep will be the ones attracting the most interest. Bayern Munich are becoming increasingly interested in Archie Gray, one of the few whose stock has risen despite the crisis.

Chelsea and Aston Villa plan to test resolve over Lucas Bergvall. Bayern and Barcelona are among the clubs in pursuit of Luka Vuskovic, a teenage centre-half on loan at Hamburg who has yet to make a competitive appearance for Spurs and is not keen to return.

Former player Ryan Mason (left) has experience of working as Spurs' caretaker boss, but hasn't been approached about replacing Tudor

Chief executive Vinai Venkatesham (pictured) was at his desk before 9am on Wednesday and locked in his office for most of the day

The sombre flight from Madrid to Stansted touched down in the early hours of Wednesday morning, by which time the Tottenham Hotspur Supporters' Trust had issued a statement branding the performance ‘a total disgrace’ and demanding a ticket refund.

Chief executive Vinai Venkatesham was at his desk before 9am and locked in his office for most of the day.

Venkatesham and sporting director Johan Lange have been thumbing through contingency ideas and making calls since the shambles against Crystal Palace last week and holding tight to the idea that it is better to avoid another change in case it sets them back a month to the point of the Frank sacking.

Former Spurs boss Harry Redknapp, among the popular options, told reporters at the Cheltenham Festival he would love to do it but had not been approached. Nor has Ryan Mason, a former Spurs player and popular coach who has twice stepped into the breach on an interim mission.

Sean Dyche tops the ‘next Spurs boss’ betting and it makes some sense, but Tottenham have always been cool on Roberto De Zerbi, another who features prominently with bookmakers because of his availability after leaving Marseille.



If they can reach the summer as a Premier League club, a limited field of candidates will open up. But for now relegation stalks the scene.

Sports.title: Iran soccer players 'rushed to new location in Australia after team member leaked hiding spot to embassy'
Sports.content:
A member of the Iranian women's soccer delegation has reneged on her decision to accept asylum in Australia, and in doing so, allegedly leaked the location of defecting Iranian players to the besieged country's embassy in Sydney.

Those players have now been moved to a safe location, Australian Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke told parliament on Wednesday.

Burke's comments came amid fallout from Team Iran's refusal to sing the country's national anthem at an Asian Cup match along the Gold Coast earlier this month. That controversial protest prompted Iranian state television to label players 'wartime traitors,' raising concern for their safety in their native country as Iran faces joint US-Israeli military attacks.

Given those safety concerns, Australia extended asylum offers to Iranian players, five of whom accepted on Monday rather than fly back to Iran with teammates.

A day later, Burke told parliament that 21-year-old striker Mohaddeseh Zolfi and support staffer Zahra Soltan Moshkehkar had also accepted the government's offer of asylum. But without specifying whether it was Zolfi or Moshkehkar who had second thoughts, Burke has since revealed one of the two changed their mind and called the Iranian embassy 'to get collected.'

That call led Iranian handlers to learn where the defectors were staying, prompting Australian officials to quickly relocate the asylum seekers.

Australia's Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke poses with Iranian women's soccer team support worker Zahra Soltan Meshkeh Kar and team player Mohaddeseh Zolfi, who were granted asylum on Tuesday. Since then, one of the two women changed her mind, according to Burke

Iran players pose for a team photo ahead of the Women's Asian Cup soccer match between Iran and the Philippines in Robina, Australia

Burke poses in an undisclosed location with five Iranian women soccer players who have been granted asylum in Australia. The women were moved after their location was leaked

'I immediately gave the instruction for people to be moved, and that's been dealt with immediately,' Burke said.

Burke also stressed that the person who decided to return to Iran was given every opportunity to remain in Australia: 'This was her decision, and every question you would want asked, was asked.'

As for the women who are staying, Burke said they will be fast-tracked to permanent residency.

Burke has identified five players as having been granted asylum: Fatemeh Pasandideh, Zahra Ghanbari, Zahra Sarbali, Atefeh Ramazanzadeh, and Mona Hamoudi.

'They want to be clear they are not political activists. They are athletes who want to be safe,' Burke said.

Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese addressed their situation on Tuesday, calling the five players 'brave women' and promising 'they're safe here and they should feel at home here.'

Meanwhile, Iran has announced it will not take part in this summer's World Cup in North America.

When asked about Iran's participation at the World Cup by Politico last week, US President Trump replied: 'I really don't care.'

Sports.title: Bayer Leverkusen poke fun at Arsenal ahead of Champions League tie with cheeky set-piece post - before scoring from one themselves!
Sports.content:
Bayer Leverkusen poke fun at Arsenal ahead of Champions League tie with cheeky set-piece post - before scoring from one themselves!

Bayer Leverkusen are preparing for a set-piece onslaught from visitors Arsenal ahead of their Champions League clash on Wednesday night, if their latest post on social media is anything to go by.

The Germans host the high-flying Premier League leaders this evening in their Round of 16 fixture with Arsenal the bookies' favourites having strolled through the league phase earlier this season.

Mikel Arteta's side have proved themselves to be ruthless competitors in both penalty areas, and have seen off countless opponents with their prowess attacking corners and free kicks.

And ahead of their meeting, Leverkusen jokingly shared an image on their X account with a sign by the corner flag reading: 'No corners allowed!!!'

'Worth a try', was the caption accompanying the post.

But it was Leverkusen who found the net from a set-piece as Robert Andrich headed home from a corner early in the second half to put the hosts ahead.

Bayer Leverkusen have jokingly claimed no corners will be allowed during tonight's Champions League game against Arsenal

The Gunners have proven adept at capitalising on set-piece opportunities this season

The Germans had earlier poked fun at Arteta's bizarre antics on Amazon Prime series All or Nothing in 2022, in which he attempted to inspire his team by showing them a shining lightbulb as an example of how 'connected' they should be.

The Leverkusen social media admin shared a video in which he proceeded to tape over every light switch in the away dressing room in a desperate attempt to unsettle the Arsenal boss.

The video has been viewed over four million times at the time of publication.

Arteta was fully focused on the challenge that faces the Gunners on Wednesday night, with huge excitement building in the fanbase as the team inch closer to a first league title since 2003-04.

'The only thing you can take is game by game and try tomorrow to be better than the opposition and earn the right to win,' he said.

'We always talk about that. Obviously we play so many games in different competitions - in the next three games we're going to play in three different competitions, against completely different opponents.



'You have to adapt to that. You have to, very early in the match, understand what the game is going to require, adapt to it and be better than them.'

Sports.title: Luke Littler lands £3,200 win on the second day of Cheltenham Festival - after darts rival Luke Humphries picked up bigger victory in first race
Sports.content:
Luke Littler bagged himself £3,200 after placing a winning bet on the second day of the Cheltenham Festival.

Just two months after earning £1million from winning the World Darts Championship, he landed a neat sum after putting his money behind King Rasko Grey in the Turners Novices' Hurdle.

Littler, 19, placed a £250 bet, so got £2,500 back in win returns and an additional £700 in place returns.

A fellow punter had put £5,000 on King Rasko Grey and so was left celebrating an astonishing £64,000 triumph.

Littler has been enjoying his time at Cheltenham alongside his beautician girlfriend Faith Millar.

Meanwhile, fellow darts star Luke Humphries hit the bullseye on day one by landing £3,250 on the very first race of the Festival.

Luke Littler celebrated a £3,200 win on the second day of Cheltenham Festival

Littler pictured at the course on Wednesday for Ladies' Day, alongside Millar

Luke Humphries with Nathan Aspinall (left), Littler and Littler's girlfriend Faith Millar

Humphries proudly displays his winning bet slip, with his friend landing an even bigger win

The 2024 world champion was in attendance with darts rivals Littler and Nathan Aspinall on Tuesday and wasted no time in bashing the bookies, placing £1,000 on Old Park Star at odds of 9/4.

And Nicky Henderson’s unbeaten hurdler obliged under jockey Nico de Boinville, coming through in the final stretch and passing Sober Glory, who had led for most of the race.

Humphries posed for a picture with racing analyst Pro Sports Advice, who had landed an even bigger prize of £17,500 after backing Old Park Star to the tune of £5,000.

Humphries, 31, confirmed his winnings would be donated to charity.

Littler, who has usurped Humphries as world No1 by landing the last two World Championship titles, was joined by his girlfriend Millar and was also pictured at the course early on day two.

Littler and Humphries will both be heading 100 miles north to Nottingham for night six of the Premier League on Thursday, where they could meet in the semi-finals.

The friends and rivals have met in the final in both of the last two seasons - winning once each - but have both had a slow start to this year's tournament.

Humphries has yet to reach a final and is currently outside the top four - who qualify for May's finals night at the O2 Arena in London. Littler sits third, having broken his duck last week in Cardiff - landing the week five trophy by seeing off Jonny Clayton.

Sports.title: Cheltenham Festival jockey claims he was 'racially abused' for being Irish by rival
Sports.content:
Cheltenham Festival jockey claims he was 'racially abused' for being Irish by rival

Amateur Declan Queally and de Boinville were involved in a heated exchange before the start of the Turners’ Novices’ Hurdle at Cheltenham on Wednesday

View 2 Images Declan Queally hit out at Nico de Boinville (Image: ITV 2026)

An Irish amateur rider accused top British jockey Nico de Boinvile of making an abusive comment towards him "of a racial nature" at Cheltenham on Wednesday. Declan Queally and de Boinville, stable jockey to Nicky Henderson, became involved in a heated exchange during a chaotic start to the Turners Novices' Hurdle, the opening race on the second day of the Cheltenham Festival.

‌



Tempers flared as the riders jostled for position among the 21 runner field which led to the starter declaring two false starts before the runners were allowed to set off. De Boinville went on to finish second on Act Of Innocence behind the Willie Mullins-trained winner King Rasko Grey while Queally finished fifth on the family-trained I'll Sort That.

‌



Cheltenham races result: Il Etait Temps survives scare to win for England World Cup winner READ MORE :

The war of words continued after the race after Queally told ITV Racing: “The start was a bit of a mess. Being abused by an English rider Nico de Boinville is not very nice. I’m an amateur. I’ve come over here riding in front of my kids and that. Horrific.” De Boinville replied: “Maybe he should look in the mirror.”

‌



At a subsequent stewards' inquiry the starter reported that Jack Kennedy, Queally, Danny Gilligan and Darragh O’Keeffe had not approached the tape at a walk or a jig-jog and thereby caused a false start. Kennedy and Queally were suspended for one day for misconduct at the Start. Gilligan and O’Keefe were suspended for two days as it was their second offence in 12 months.

View 2 Images Nico De Boinville was accused of abuse another jockey (Image: ITV )

‌



The report also said that Queally made a complaint "regarding an incident at the start". Queally told the Racing Post that de Boinville "called me a f*****g p***k a couple of times in front of the ITV camera".

He later told Mirror Racing: "It was of a racial nature. I’ve reported it and it is being dealt with by the BHA stewards." De Boinville was contacted for a response and said he had no comment to make.

The stewards notice said. "The stewards, having heard the initial evidence from Queally and Nico de Boinville, have adjourned the matter to obtain further evidence."

Article continues below

Sports.title: Harry Redknapp lifted lid on relationship with Sir Alex Ferguson after Cheltenham winners
Sports.content:
Harry Redknapp lifted lid on relationship with Sir Alex Ferguson after Cheltenham winners

Former Premier League opponents Harry Redknapp and Sir Alex Ferguson have continued their rivalry in racing as they descend on Cheltenham once more

View 2 Images Harry Redknapp showed there's no animosity between him and Sir Alex Ferguson these days after a special Cheltenham day for the duo (Image: AFP/Getty Images)

Former Tottenham and West Ham boss Harry Redknapp has an extensive history going up against Sir Alex Ferguson in the dugout. However, the retired tactician showed his old adversary nothing but love after each got their first Cheltenham wins.

‌



Manchester United great Ferguson, 84, had a bumper day when two of his horses, Monmiral and Protektorat, won back-to-back races for the Scot. Redknapp, 79, joined in the celebrations when Shakem Up’Arry won the Plate Handicap Chase, and he made sure to include his fellow Premier League legend as he paid tribute.

‌



"To be here now in this position is incredible," he said following his landmark Cheltenham win. "My main worry is that they [the horses] get round safe, I jump every jump with them and think, ‘Please don’t fall or get hurt’. When they come back safe and sound, especially in front, it’s all the better.

‌



"It’s great for Sir Alex to have a winner as well, we had some great days. Going into his office at Old Trafford at quarter past two when the teams were out and we’d watch a bit of racing for 15 minutes and then we’d go out and I’d do my best to try and pull off a miracle and get a result!

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

"We both loved the racing and we both loved the football. For him to get two winners today is great and for me to get a winner is fantastic."

‌



Jockey hit with suspension over incident in horse race as stewards issue statement READ MORE :

Monmiral caused a major upset at odds of 25-1 when he took the Pertemps Final for Ferguson's breakthrough win. The Old Trafford icon had owned horses for many years prior to that triumph but had never managed to celebrate a Cheltenham winner.

A great day then got even better as Protektorat topped the podium in the Ryanair Chase. And the Scot could hardly believe his luck as his racing investments started to take flight, with Caldwell Potter adding to his Cheltenham triumphs in 2025.

FREE William Hill Shop Bets inside your Mirror every day of Cheltenham Don't miss a FREE shop bet inside your favourite racing paper every day of the Cheltenham festival! Plus, get expert inside from our top tipsters with pullouts every day from Tuesday March 10 - Friday March 13, 2026 covering the runners, riders, and everything you need to make the 102nd Cheltenham festival the biggest ever. Find out more here. Bet 18+ only. Terms and conditions apply. See paper for details.

‌



“I’ve finally had a winner here. Two in 40 minutes! It’s unbelievable, isn’t it? It’s a special place," said Ferguson, who is being slated for more success this year. "It has been probably 20 years that I’ve wanted to have a winner here, as when I first started I just had Flat horses and no National Hunt ones.

“I got interested in the jumps with Ged [Mason] and [the late] John [Hales]. It 100 per cent makes it more enjoyable having a winner with friends. Everyone looks forward to Cheltenham, while on the Flat you have the Derby. These two events are unsurpassed.

View 2 Images Redknapp is an avid racing enthusiast, just like Ferguson (Image: AP)

Article continues below

“It’s special here, it’s like the Derby or the FA Cup final and I’ve waited a while to experience this. A lot of people who buy horses have never had a winner, never mind here. It’s a feeling of elation.

“Of course it’s not the same as winning at football, that was my life, I was immersed in that, this is what I do for pleasure so it’s a different feeling. I don’t have to worry about it, I leave that to the trainer.”

Sports.title: Arsenal plot Tino Livramento move as Mikel Arteta aims to take aim of Newcastle impasse
Sports.content:
Arsenal plot Tino Livramento move as Mikel Arteta aims to take aim of Newcastle impasse

Arsenal are in the midst of a challenge for the Premier League title but Mikel Arteta is already putting thought into potential additions for next season with a Manchester City target on their list

View 2 Images Livramento will have just two years left on his deal at the end of the season (Image: Getty Images)

Arsenal are keeping tabs on Newcastle defender Tino Livramento as the Gunners plot major summer additions. The North London side are in the midst of a battle for this year’s Premier League crown.

‌



Having put together a seven-point advantage over Manchester City, they are currently favourites to lift the crown. And while there is still work to be done for this season, it appears that Arsenal are already putting thought into a potential title defence.

‌



As per The Telegraph, Newcastle star Livramento is being considered as a potential summer signing. The 23-year-old will have just two years left on his contract at St James’ Park and as yet, talks over an extended stay have been shelved.

‌



Newcastle are keen on Livramento staying but they recognise that his contractual situation could potentially see his value drop. The defender also has the same representatives as Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta, which could see the Gunners move into prime position for his services should he decide to leave.

FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Arsenal's double transfer plan to hamper young star as England World Cup door closes READ MORE :

Arsenal offered Julian Alvarez transfer hope as Atletico Madrid star casts doubt over future READ MORE :

Newcastle are expected to demand around £60m for the defender should he be moved on, albeit Eddie Howe has already confirmed he wants to keep him around.

‌



Speaking earlier this season amid reported interest from Manchester City, he said: “Yes, I think it goes without saying [that we want to keep Livramento]. I think it's frustrating for us that we're consistently talking about players, rumoured to be leaving.

“We want to go the other way. We want to strengthen the group. We want to make our team and squad stronger for the future rather than lose our best players.”

View 2 Images Tino Livramento is being eyed by Arsenal (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Meanwhile, Livramento has also expressed his happiness with life with Newcastle. He said: ”The goal was always to be playing in the Premier League as early as possible. I left Chelsea and got that experience straight away at Southampton. I had some tough times with injuries but the club and the fans were great to me.

“Now I’m at a place I absolutely love – the atmosphere, the supporters, the manager, it feels like I’m in a great place at Newcastle. I’m in the best place I could be to learn and develop as a footballer.”

Should Arsenal move for Livramento, that could see Ben White’s future at the club left in question. The former Brighton man already finds himself behind Jurrien Timber for the right-back berth, while he will only have two years left on his deal heading into next season.

Sports.title: Every country to withdraw from World Cup as Iran boycott USA 2026 in explosive statement
Sports.content:
Every country to withdraw from World Cup as Iran boycott USA 2026 in explosive statement

Iran's sports minister has confirmed that the nation will boycott this summer's 2026 World Cup in North America amid the escalating tensions in the Middle East

View 2 Images Iran had qualified for the World Cup this summer but are now set to boycott the tournament (Image: Getty Images)

Iran will not compete at this summer's World Cup due to the ongoing war against Israel and the United States, the nation's sports minister has said. There had been concerns that Iran would boycott the tournament due to the escalating tensions in the Middle East.

‌



The tournament takes place in America, Canada and Mexico in the summer. Iran have been drawn in Group G with New Zealand, Belgium and Egypt, with the tournament due to kick off on June 11. However, the sports minister, Ahmad Donyamal, has revealed in a television interview that they plan to boycott.

‌



"Since this corrupt government assassinated our leader, we have no conditions under which we can participate in the World Cup," he said. "In view of the malicious measures taken against Iran, two wars were forced upon us within eight or nine months, and several thousand of our people were killed. Therefore, we definitely have no possibility of participating in this way."

‌



FIFA statement on cancelling World Cup as Iran withdraw and Donald Trump speaks READ MORE :

World Cup in crisis as nation officially confirms they will boycott tournament READ MORE :

Iran's call to exit the tournament before a ball has been kicked will pose a significant problem for FIFA, as no qualified nation has ever boycotted the World Cup for political reasons.

Following confirmation of Iran's decision, Mirror Football takes a look at the past instances of countries withdrawing from the World Cup due to political circumstances.

Uruguay - 1934

Reigning World Champions headed to the second edition of the tournament in 1934 following their success four years prior. However, they pulled out in protest at several European teams not travelling to South America for the 1930 World Cup in Uruguay.

‌



The 1934 World Cup remains the only one where the defending champions didn't participate.

Austria - 1938

One of the tournament favourites for the 1938 tournament, Austria, couldn't participate due to the Nazi forces annexing the country at the time. Some Austrian stars joined the German squad, though star player Matthias Sindelar declined to represent the Nazi-led nation.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



India - 1950

In the one time that India managed to qualify for the World Cup, they opted not to attend. In 1950, the Asian country advanced to the finals by default due to withdrawals from qualifiying.

Their absences was down to travel expenses, training time and team selection issues.

African teams - 1966

The 1966 tournament is fondly remembered in England, but one aspect that often goes unnoticed is the absence of African teams. The continent's nations boycotted the qualifying rounds in protest against FIFA's decision to allocate only one spot for the African, Asian and Oceania confederations.

‌



View 2 Images 30th July 1966: Geoff Hurst scores England's third goal against West Germany in the World Cup final at Wembley Stadium. The goal, awarded upon the judgement of the Russian linesman has remained one of the most controversial goals in the history of the competition. England became World champions with a 4-2 victory after extra time. (Photo by Hulton Archive/Getty Images)

Chile - 1974

In 1974, we witnessed a qualified nation nearly boycot the World Cup on political grounds. In the intercontinetial play-off between Chile and the Soviet Union, the latter declinded to take part in the fixture in protest of Augusto Pinochet's dictatorship.

He had toppled the socialist government, transforming Chile from a former Soviet ally into an adversary. Consequently, the Chilean players entered the pitch alone, with captain Francisco Valdes netting into an empty goal before the match was called off.

Article continues below

2022 World Cup protests

In more recent years, there have been very few boycott threats, but tensions did emerge during the 2022 World Cup in Qatar. All 32 qualified teams participated in the tournament, but there were demonstrations from some players concerning alleged human rights violations and the nation's controversial LGBT record.

Sports.title: Man Utd handed timely injury boost for Aston Villa game after key player's setback
Sports.content:
Man Utd handed timely injury boost for Aston Villa game after key player's setback

Manchester United are poised to have Noussair Mazraoui fit for their mammoth game with Aston Villa this Sunday - but Lisandro Martinez is due to remain sidelined

View 2 Images Noussair Mazraoui looks to have shaken an injury issue and is due to be available on Sunday (Image: Getty Images)

Manchester United could be set to welcome Noussair Mazraoui back as they prepare for their crunch clash with Aston Villa - which could have huge ramifications for the Champions League spots.

‌



Mazraoui was forced off late on in the loss at Newcastle, which left him in a race against time to be fit for the clash with the Midlands outfit at Old Trafford. He is now back training and looks like he'll be available to Michael Carrick.

‌



The game at St James' Park, the first loss of the Carrick era, was the Moroccan's first start for the Red Devils since he returned from AFCON. He had been a regular for his country as they reached the final, eventually losing to Senegal in controversial circumstances.

‌



The ex-Bayern Munich man has forced his way back into the fold and will be among those in contention to play on the right side of the defence.

It is a welcome boost for Carrick, who will be be without Lisandro Martinez after the defender suffered a setback in training. The Argentine has missed the past three Premier League outings with a calf injury and is unlikely to feature against Villa as the club take precautions.

Sir Jim Ratcliffe has only two years left to achieve Man Utd promise with over £300m gulf READ MORE :

The centre-back had partnered with Harry Maguire in the first five matches of Carrick's second coming, but the World Cup winner had spent 10 months on the sidelines following a cruciate ligament injury, thus the medical staff want to tread carefully after the Daily Mail reported he had suffered a ‘slight reaction’.

‌



Leny Yoro has come into the side to replace Martinez for those fixtures - and the young Frenchman is expected keep his place for Sunday's home game against Villa, especially with next to no more fit centre-halves available. Matthijs de Ligt is also injured with youngster Ayden Heaven likely sitting on the bench on Sunday.

View 2 Images Lisandro Martinez's injury issue is set to keep him sidelined (Image: PA)

United were beaten 2-1 in this fixture when the teams played at Villa Park earlier this season, but the fortunes of both teams have changed notably since then. The Red Devils now sit in third, ahead of Unai Emery's side on goal difference.

Article continues below

Villa had been talked up as outsiders for the title back in December, but since the turn of the year their form has fallen off a cliff. They now face the realistic possibility of slipping out the top five and missing out on the Champions League. Chelsea recently hammered them 4-1 with Emery overseeing a run of three wins in 11 games.

Sports.title: Cheltenham tips: Thursday's best bets for EVERY race from our top tipster Newsboy
Sports.content:
Cheltenham tips: Thursday's best bets for EVERY race from our top tipster Newsboy

Newsboy's race by race guide includes a 1-2-3 for seven races on day three of the four-day Cheltenham Festival which has the Stayers' Hurdle as its feature

View 7 Images Newsboy has made his picks for the Cheltenham Festival (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

OLDSCHOOL OUTLAW (1.20) beat Bambino Fever when the pair met at Naas in December - and there’s less between the pair than the betting might have us believe. Placed in bumpers in the first three months of 2025 for Co Kildare-based Garry Caldwell, Oldschool Outlaw joined Gordon Elliott after finishing seventh at Aintree in April.

‌



Walk In The Park’s daughter started out for her new stable in a Listed maiden hurdle for mares at Navan in November and scored by six and a half lengths. She then met last year’s Champion Bumper heroine Bambino Fever in a maiden hurdle at Naas and came out on top by half a length, the front two 11 lengths clear of the third.

‌



Cheltenham festival jockey suspended over false starts on first day READ MORE :

That performance caught the eye of JP McManus and Oldschool Outlaw carried the green-and-gold livery when completing her path to today with a nine-length supremacy over Place De La Nation in the Grade 3 Solerina Mares Novice Hurdle at Fairyhouse at the beginning of last month.

‌



There is plenty more where that came from. Hot favourite Bambino Fever, with Festival ‘previous’ on her CV, has to be feared, with Echoing Silence and Charme De Faust the most convincing candidates for the final place on the podium.

NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 OLDSCHOOL OUTLAW, 2 BAMBINO FEVER, 3 ECHOING SILENCE

View 7 Images Jordans Cross: Newsboy's pick (Image: PA)

‌



JORDANS CROSS (2.00) is unbeaten over fences when staying upright, and another good account is on the cards.

Successful in a point-to-point at Quakerstown in Co Clare in March 2024, the son of Berkshire started out for Anthony Honeyball with a second in a Chepstow bumper nine months later.

Having gone one better in a maiden hurdle at Southwell in December, Jordans Cross ran second under a penalty at Doncaster two days before the turn of the year and was course for 301 days before going chasing this season.

‌



My selection marked his debut in the sphere with a two-and-a-half-length winning margin at Aintree at the end of October and was challenging for the lead when coming down at the final fence behind Kdeux Saint Fray at Cheltenham weeks later.

But he was back in business with a nose call from Go West at Doncaster 12 days before Christmas. And Jordans Cross made it three wins from three completed starts over the larger obstacles with another minimum-margin verdict from Quebecois in the novices’ handicap chase over course and distance on Cheltenham’s Trials Day card in January. Sam Twiston-Davies’ partner is another 6lb higher today, but has more to give and must go well.

In an open, full-field contest, Meetmebythesea looks primed to go well, while Wingmen and Sixmilebridge are other names to throw into the melting pot.

‌



NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 JORDANS CROSS, 2 MEETMEBYTHESEA, 3 WINGMEN

View 7 Images Wodhooh: fancied for the Mares' Hurdle (Image: PA)

WODHOOH (2.40) saw her burden lightened when Lossiemouth went for the Champion Hurdle - and should make no mistake.

‌



Gordon Elliott’s mare has been a winning machine over hurdles - she’s lost just once in 10 starts in the sphere.

A maiden from four attempts on the Flat for Sir Michael Stoute, Wodhooh was promoted to first place when making her obstacles debut at Listowel in September 2023 but needed no further intervention from the stewards to finish her juvenile campaign a perfect 5-5.

Sent into handicaps last season, my selection carried on the good work with a beating of Joyeuse over this course and distance 11 days before Christmas 2024 and returned to the Cotswolds to lift the Martin Pipe Conditional Jockeys’ Handicap Hurdle here last March, thumping Act Of Authority by four and a quarter lengths.

‌



A first defeat at the hands of Lossiemouth in the Grade 1 Aintree Hurdle the following month was no disgrace, but normal service has been resumed this term.

Wodhooh marked her comeback by landing the Grade 2 Ascot Hurdle in November and yet another personal best - a two-and-a-quarter-length margin from Feet Of A Dancer in a Leopardstown Grade 3 over Christmas - gives Jack Kennedy’s mount a class edge.

Last year’s runner-up Jade De Grugy looks destined for the silver medal once more, with Feet Of A Dancer and Take No Chances fighting it out for the bronze.

‌



NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 WODHOOH, 2 JADE DE GRUGY, 3 FEET OF A DANCER

View 7 Images Ma Shantou: Stayer's Hurdle pick (Image: Alan Crowhurst, Getty Images)

MA SHANTOU (3.20) takes on the toughest assignment of his career - but is expected to prove up to the job.

‌



Emma Lavelle handled the career of 2019 Stayers’ Hurdle hero Paisley Park with consummate skill - and in Ma Shantou the trainer has another top grinder on her hands. Successful in two of his five attempts as a novice last season - he scored at Warwick and

Windsor in the run-up to Christmas 2024 - Shantou’s son finished the campaign with a seventh to Jasmin De Vaux in the Albert Bartlett Novices’ Hurdle at the Festival 12 months ago.

But the seven-year-old has made great strides this winter, returning to action with a handicap score under Harry Cobden here in October.

‌



Ma Shantou was below par when favourite for a similar event at Haydock Park four weeks later, but since then it has been gravy all the way.

Staying in the handicap sphere, my selection bounced back to beat Ace Of Spades over this course and distance on New Year’s Day.

And he made light of the move back up in class to land a punt in the Grade 2 Cleeve Hurdle on Cheltenham’s Trials Day fixture at the end of January, storming up the hill to hold Impose Toi by seven lengths.

‌



The evidence of that performance is that Ma Shantou isn’t finished yet, and he’s the choice for a fourth - and most important yet - victory at the home of National Hunt racing.

In a stellar running of the Stayers’ Hurdle, Teahupoo, first and second in 2024 and 2025, reigning champion Bob Olinger and Honesty Policy command respect, along with regular attender Home By The Lee.

NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 MA SHANTOU, 2 TEAHUPOO, 3 HONESTY POLICY

‌



View 7 Images Fact To File can defend the Ryanair Chase

FACT TO FILE (4.00) is rightly a warm order to keep hold of the crown he captured in bloodless fashion 12 months ago.

Many will perceive a lack of ambition in the move to come back to the Ryanair Chase rather than seek glory in the Gold Cup - but fortune doesn’t always favour the brave.

‌



Fact To File spared his backers an anxious moment when striding away to justify 6-4 favouritism in this race last March, and it should be a similar story for the Willie Mullins trainee this time around.

Although he was well below form behind Marine Nationale back at two miles at Punchestown in April, JP McManus’s nine-year-old lost nothing in defeat but first prize when a neck second to stablemate Gaelic Warrior in the Grade 1 John Durkan Memorial Punchestown Chase on his return.

FREE William Hill Shop Bets inside your Mirror every day of Cheltenham Don't miss a FREE shop bet inside your favourite racing paper every day of the Cheltenham festival! Plus, get expert inside from our top tipsters with pullouts every day from Tuesday March 10 - Friday March 13, 2026 covering the runners, riders, and everything you need to make the 102nd Cheltenham festival the biggest ever. Find out more here. Bet 18+ only. Terms and conditions apply. See paper for details.

‌



My selection didn’t shine when sixth behind The Jukebox Man in the top-level King George VI Chase at Kempton Park on Boxing Day, but his win in the Irish Gold Cup at Leopardstown last time out is right up there with his career highlights.

Never far from the action, Mark Walsh’s mount strode to the front on the home turn and wasn’t for catching, extending his lead to five lengths from Gaelic Warrior by the time he got to the finish line.

An emphatic winner of the Brown Advisory Novices’ Chase here two years ago, Fact To File should rack up a Festival hat-trick.

‌



King George second Banbridge deserves to be high on the list of dangers, followed by Jonbon and last year’s runner-up Heart Wood.

NEWBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 FACT TO FILE, 2 BANBRIDGE, 3 JONBON

View 7 Images Absolutely Doyen: has been a revelation for Paul Nicholls (Image: PA)

‌



ABSOLUTELY DOYEN (4.40) is unbeaten in five starts for Paul Nicholls - and there’s no reason why he shouldn’t show to the fore today.

Doyen’s son failed to find the winner’s enclosure in five outings in point-to-points and three starts over hurdles for Fergal O’Brien.

But the switch to Paul Nicholls has seen a new horse emerge, starting with a victory at odds of 1-7 against three opponents at Stratford in October.

‌



Absolutely Doyen doubled up in a Sedgefield novice hurdle the following month before stepping into the handicap sphere to rack up the hat-trick at Ascot.

The six-year-old kept up the winning run when landing a qualifier for today at Wincanton on Boxing Day, and the nap hand arrived via a three-and-three-quarter-length humbling in a novices’ hurdle at Musselburgh - Absolutely Doyen carried an 8lb penalty - last time.

This test is obviously more demanding, but it will be a surprise if the 14-time champion trainer can’t tease out further progress from this going-places performer.

‌



In a headscratcher of a Pertemps Final, favourite Supremely West, C’Est Different and the first-time-blinkered Melbourne Shamrock are other names to throw into the mix.

NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 ABSOLUTELY DOYEN, 2 SUPREMELY WEST, 3 MELBOURNE SHAMROCK

View 7 Images Herakles Westwood is Newsboy's choice (Image: Getty Images)

‌



HERAKLES WESTWOOD (5.20) brings plenty of Cheltenham form to the table - and the booking of top amateur Finny Maguire is another plus.

Warren Greatrex saddled the nine-year-old to novice handicap chase wins at Newbury and Windsor last season, but Saddler Maker’s son, seventh in the 2025 National Hunt Chase before a fifth at Punchestown, has been kept to the home of jump racing in three outings since.

Interestingly, Maguire was on board when Herakles Westwood made his return in a handicap chase for amateur riders here in November and ran second to Hung Jury.

‌



My preference lost a shoe but came home well with a fourth to Blaze The Way over this course and distance 13 days before Christmas, but was back to winning form over a furlong shorter here on New Year’s Day.

Sent off at 11-2 in a field of eight, Herakles Westwood had more in hand than the official margin of victory might suggest when holding Katate Dori by half a length.

Under the circumstances, a 2lb nudge up the handicap could have been worse. In a fiendishly difficult close to Wednesday’s fun, Waterford Whispers, Daily Present, Weveallbeencaught and Ask Brewster are just four horses who merit a second look.

Article continues below

NEWSBOY’S 1-2-3: 1 HERAKLES WESTWOOD, 2 WATERFORD WHISPERS, 3 WEVEALLBEENCAUGHT

Sports.title: Cheltenham jockeys furious as Ruby Walsh calls for immediate intervention - 'It's a joke'
Sports.content:
Cheltenham jockeys furious as Ruby Walsh calls for immediate intervention - 'It's a joke'

Ruby Walsh and several jockeys expressed frustration with race starts at Cheltenham Festival Day 2 on Wednesday, with the racing legend calling for the British Horseracing Authority to step in

View 2 Images Ruby Walsh wants to see a change at Cheltenham (Image: ITV Racing)

Multiple jockeys voiced their dissatisfaction with race starts at the Cheltenham Festival on Wednesday. The second day of the prestigious meeting was marred by complaints from riders about the starters' procedures before each race.

‌



King Rasko Grey, trained by Willie Mullins and ridden by Paul Townsend, triumphed in the opening Novices' Hurdle. Harry Cobden then guided Kitzbuhel to victory in the second race of the day, the Novices' Chase, securing consecutive wins for Mullins to begin proceedings.

‌



Jingko Blue subsequently claimed the Handicap Hurdle before Final Orders prevailed in the Cross Country Chase. Throughout the afternoon, however, there was evident discontent amongst jockeys regarding the starters' instructions at the beginning of races.

‌



ITV were covering the second day of the festival, with presenter Matt Chapman disclosing that he had spoken to several jockeys following their races. Chapman shared comments from four jockeys who had apparently spoken about the race starts at the festival.

Cheltenham horse HMS Seahorse dies as Festival rocked by tragic news READ MORE :

Cheltenham Festival jockey row explodes as amateur claims 'abuse' in remarkable TV rant READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! All the best sports news and much more on our dedicated Facebook page

"They let him go when we weren't ready, it's just a joke," Chapman claims Mark Walsh said, who finished 14th aboard Free Spirit in the Novices' Hurdle.

‌



Chapman then added that Jack Kennedy said: "It's too tight at the start. There are too many runners, it's too tight and we're being packed together."

Brian Hughes reportedly had stern words about the race starts on day two. Chapman reported that Hughes told him: "Horrific, horrendous. The outside horses are cantering in, everyone on the inside are jogging in, the ones on the inside basically have no chance."

View 2 Images There have been frustrations with the start line at Cheltenham (Image: Tom Jenkins, Getty Images)

‌



Gavin Sheehan, jockey for Wendigo who fell in the Brown Advisory Novices' Chase, allegedly told Chapman: "We are saying no and the starter says go."

Racing icon Ruby Walsh echoed the sentiments of the jockeys and urged the British Horseracing Authority to intervene.

"The BHA [British Horseracing Authority] need to give their starters the right mechanisms to start races," Walsh told ITV. "A quarter of this field had no chance."
